purpose: Second AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering processor routing and memory toggle behavior.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular processor node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular processor node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular processor node.

layers.summary: A left-memory processor node rotates an incoming three-channel signal from input tuple positions 0, 1, 2 to output positions 2, 0, 1, then toggles memory.
layers.boundary: This layer states symbolic processor routing and memory toggle only; it does not assert physical circulator behavior.

layers.summary: The processor node is represented using the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the wire trace.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete GELC processor circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one fixed-role processor activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the existing AS Universal Cell probe and adds no new transition semantics.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware remains a candidate interpretation of the processor node, not verified AS hardware.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation unverified and separates processor behavior from RALA-like auxiliary reconfiguration.
